title: Ensure that we still return 1, and print something to stderr, on (skopeo copy) failure
body:
https://github.com/projectatomic/skopeo/pull/519 made `skopeo copy` suceed and print nothing to stderr; that could lead to hard-to-diagnose failures in rare corner cases, e.g. shell scripts which do
`skopeo copy $src $dst` (as opposed to the correct `skopeo copy "$src" "$dst"` ) if `$src` and `$dst` are empty due to a previous failure.
